title: Hero player uses glass-code-block showing registry usage
task: Switch the hero's Remotion player from browser-flow to the glass-code-block component, and feed it a `code` snippet that best demonstrates how to use the remocn registry (install via shadcn CLI + compose real registry components in a Remotion scene). Keep the bg.jpg scene background for continuity.

stories.title: Render glass-code-block in the hero with demonstrative registry code
acceptanceCriteria: hero.tsx uses registry["glass-code-block"] instead of registry["browser-flow"] for the hero player entry

A demonstrative `code` string is passed via inputProps that shows the real remocn workflow: the shadcn CLI install command for remocn components (as a comment) AND importing + composing actual registry components (blur-reveal and mesh-gradient-bg, which both exist in registry/remocn/) inside a Remotion scene
The code references only components that actually exist in the registry (no fictional component names) and uses correct prop names (e.g. BlurReveal text=...)
inputProps no longer passes browser-flow-only props (url); analytics component labels referencing the preview are updated to 'glass-code-block'
The bg.jpg scene background is preserved by passing glass-code-block's `background` prop (image + subtle dark overlay) so the glass card floats over the texture
glass card sizing (width/height/fontSize/title) is set so all code lines fit inside the card without overflow at the 1280x720 canvas; component stays type-correct with no unused imports
